schedule.daily_routine: Up around 6:15am, quick breakfast and levothyroxine, commute to office; lunch is usually a short walk and emails; evenings rotate between class, studying, and family time; tries to be in bed by 10:30pm with CPAP, but late-night studying sometimes pushes bedtime later and worsens reflux.

projects: Preparing 1120S and partnership returns for multiple clients
Month-end close support for a retained CFO-services client
Implementing a standardized workpaper template and review checklist for the team
Training a new associate on SALT basics and client communications
professional.performance_issues: Occasional deadline pressure due to evening classes; has requested limited late-night client calls and is trying to front-load work before exam weeks, which has caused minor friction during busy periods.
workplace_relationships: Direct manager: Priya Nair (supportive but expects proactive scheduling around busy season)
Peer mentor: Thomas “TJ” Ramirez (helps cover client questions when Nikolai is in class)

Cooking simple stir-fries and meal prep on Sundays
Learning data analytics tools (Excel Power Query, basic Python) for school and work
habits: Uses CPAP most nights; skips occasionally when traveling or after late study sessions (worsens daytime fatigue)
Limits coffee after noon to reduce reflux and sleep disruption
Keeps antacids in his work bag for GERD flare-ups
Tracks assignments and deadlines in a color-coded calendar
Occasional late-night snacking while studying, which he is trying to stop
lifestyle.living_situation: Rents a two-bedroom apartment in North Austin with his partner; daughter stays every other weekend and one weeknight, so the second bedroom doubles as a study space and a room for Ava.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Tries to follow a reflux-friendly diet on weekdays (smaller meals, fewer spicy/fried foods); avoids eating within 2–3 hours of bedtime; aims for higher fiber and lean protein but lapses during crunch weeks.
